name: GNATCOLL.Geometry

documentation.description: A set of planar geometric utilities (intersections of segments, etc).

A rectangle has sides parallel to the two axis of the space coordinates.
It is defined by its top-left and bottom-right corners. If you need a
more general definition of a rectangle, use the generic algorithms on
polygons.

array_types.name: Polygon
array_types.code: type Polygon   is array (Positive range <>) of Point;

array_types.name: Segment
array_types.code: type Segment   is array (1 .. 2) of Point;

record_types.name: Circle
Point

documentation.description: General representation for a point in 2D

subtypes.name: Distance_Type
subtypes.code: subtype Distance_Type is Coordinate'Base range 0.0 .. Coordinate'Base'Last;

documentation.description:
Constant used to indicate that an infinity number of points would match,
for instance in the case of the intersection of coincident lines

Constant used to indicate that the point doesn't exist (no intersection
for instance)

subprograms.code: function "-" (P2, P1 : Point) return Vector

documentation.description: Return the vector to go from P1 to P2.

subprograms.name: Area
subprograms.code: function Area (Self : Polygon)  return Distance_Type

documentation.description:
Return the area of the (possibly non-convex) polygon. For the triangle,
the area will be negative if the vertices are oriented clockwise

subprograms.name: Area
subprograms.code: function Area (Self : Triangle) return Distance_Type

documentation.description:
Return the area of the (possibly non-convex) polygon. For the triangle,
the area will be negative if the vertices are oriented clockwise

subprograms.name: Bisector
subprograms.code: function Bisector (S : Segment) return Line

documentation.description:
Return the bisector to S, i.e. the line that is perpendicular to S and
goes through its middle.

subprograms.name: Centroid
subprograms.code: function Centroid (Self : Polygon) return Point

documentation.description: Return the centroid of the polygon (aka center of gravity).

subprograms.name: Cross
subprograms.code: function Cross (Vector1, Vector2 : Vector) return Coordinate

documentation.description:
Return the magnitude of the cross-product of the two vectors.
Technically, this is also a vector, but since we are in 2D, this is
represented as a scalar.

In 2D, this is also the value of  |Vector1| * |Vector2| * sin (alpha).
This is positive if Vector1 is less than 180 degrees clockwise from B.

Last, in 2D the cross product is also the area of the parallelogram with
two of its side formed by Vector1 and Vector2.
       ----A---
       \       \
        B       |
         \       \
          ---------

subprograms.name: Distance
subprograms.code: function Distance (From : Point; To : Line)    return Distance_Type

documentation.description: Return the distance between P and the second parameter.

subprograms.name: Distance
subprograms.code: function Distance (From : Point; To : Point)   return Distance_Type

documentation.description: Return the distance between P and the second parameter.

subprograms.name: Distance
subprograms.code: function Distance (From : Point; To : Polygon) return Distance_Type

documentation.description: Return the distance between P and the second parameter.

subprograms.name: Distance
subprograms.code: function Distance (From : Point; To : Segment) return Distance_Type

documentation.description: Return the distance between P and the second parameter.

subprograms.name: Dot
subprograms.code: function Dot (Vector1, Vector2 : Vector) return Coordinate

documentation.description:
Return the dot product of Vector1 and Vector2. Mathematically, this is
also the value of |Vector1| * |Vector2| * cos (alpha), where alpha is
the angle between the two vectors. When Dot is 0, the two vectors are
orthogonal or null.

subprograms.name: Inside
subprograms.code: function Inside (P : Point; L    : Line)     return Boolean

documentation.description: True if P is on the segment or line

subprograms.name: Inside
subprograms.code: function Inside (P : Point; Poly : Polygon)  return Boolean

documentation.description: True if P is on the segment or line

subprograms.name: Inside
subprograms.code: function Inside (P : Point; S    : Segment)  return Boolean

documentation.description: True if P is on the segment or line

subprograms.name: Inside
subprograms.code: function Inside (P : Point; T    : Triangle) return Boolean

documentation.description: True if P is on the segment or line

subprograms.name: Intersect
subprograms.code: function Intersect (C1, C2 : Circle)      return Boolean

documentation.description: Whether the two parameters intersect

subprograms.name: Intersect
subprograms.code: function Intersect (L : Line; C : Circle) return Boolean

documentation.description: Whether the two parameters intersect

subprograms.name: Intersect
subprograms.code: function Intersect (R1, R2 : Rectangle)   return Boolean

documentation.description: Whether the two parameters intersect

subprograms.name: Intersect
subprograms.code: function Intersect (T1, T2 : Triangle)    return Boolean

documentation.description: Whether the two parameters intersect

subprograms.name: Intersection
subprograms.code: function Intersection (L1, L2 : Line)    return Point

documentation.description:
Return the intersection of the two parameters. The result is either a
simple point, or No_Point when they don't intersect, or
Infinity_Points when the two parameters intersect on an infinity of
points.

subprograms.name: Intersection
subprograms.code: function Intersection (S1, S2 : Segment) return Point

documentation.description:
Return the intersection of the two parameters. The result is either a
simple point, or No_Point when they don't intersect, or
Infinity_Points when the two parameters intersect on an infinity of
points.

subprograms.name: Length
subprograms.code: function Length (Vect : Vector) return Distance_Type

documentation.description: Return the magnitude of the vector

subprograms.name: Same_Side
subprograms.code: function Same_Side (P1, P2 : Point; As : Line)    return Boolean

documentation.description:
Whether the two points lay on the same side of the line overlapping
the segment. It is slightly faster to use the Segment version

subprograms.name: Same_Side
subprograms.code: function Same_Side (P1, P2 : Point; As : Segment) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Whether the two points lay on the same side of the line overlapping
the segment. It is slightly faster to use the Segment version

subprograms.name: To_Circle
subprograms.code: function To_Circle (P1, P2, P3 : Point) return Circle

documentation.description:
Return the circle that passes through the 3 points. If the 3 points are
colinear, No_Circle is returned.

subprograms.name: To_Line
subprograms.code: function To_Line (P1, P2 : Point) return Line

documentation.description:
Return the line going through the two points, or overlapping the
segment.

subprograms.name: To_Line
subprograms.code: function To_Line (Seg : Segment) return Line

documentation.description:
Return the line going through the two points, or overlapping the
segment.

subprograms.name: To_Vector
subprograms.code: function To_Vector (S : Segment) return Vector

documentation.description:
Return the vector that indicates the direction and magnitude of the
segment.

The type used to represent coordinates and distances.
Distances are returned as a subtype including only the positive (or
null) range, so this type must include positive numbers.
